prompt: The following VCF record contains imputed genotypes. Compare the hard-call genotype (GT) with the genotype probabilities (GP) for sample IRIS_220. Is the hard call consistent with the GP values? What is the imputation quality (DR2) for this site?
context:
#CHROM	POS	ID	REF	ALT	QUAL	FILTER	INFO	FORMAT	IRIS_220	IRIS_221
chr01	34812509	.	G	A	.	.	DR2=0.41;AF=0.09	GT:DS:GP	0/0:0.48:0.55,0.42,0.03	0/1:0.97:0.08,0.87,0.05
correct_answer: For IRIS_220: GT=0/0 but GP shows P(0/0)=0.55, P(0/1)=0.42, P(1/1)=0.03. The hard call is technically consistent (0/0 has the highest probability) but the confidence is very low -- only 55% vs 42% for het. The dosage DS=0.48 reflects this uncertainty (would be 0.0 for a confident 0/0). DR2=0.41, indicating poor imputation quality at this site.
rubric: Must note GT=0/0 is technically the highest-probability call but with very low confidence (0.55 vs 0.42). Must extract DR2=0.41 and note it indicates poor imputation quality. Must connect DS=0.48 to the uncertainty.
notes: Tests understanding of discordance between hard calls and probabilistic genotype fields in imputed data.
